title: Move Config and Database Credentials Outside Web Root
description:
Sensitive configuration files (`dbconnection.php`, `config.php`) containing database credentials were stored inside the `public_html` web root, making them potentially accessible via HTTP requests. These files need to be relocated outside the web root to prevent unauthorized access or information disclosure.

## Requirements

- Move `dbconnection.php` and `config.php` out of the `public_html/apps` folder.
- Use a non-standard file extension (e.g., `.custom`) to prevent direct execution.
- Update all `include_once` references to use relative paths navigating above the web root.
- Set appropriate file permissions to restrict access.

## Resolution

This task was ultimately deferred to the Laravel migration, which uses Laravel's built-in `.env` environment file functionality to manage all application-level passwords and tokens securely.
acceptance_criteria: Database credentials are not stored inside the public_html directory
Configuration files use non-standard extensions to prevent direct execution
Application code correctly loads config from outside the web root
File permissions restrict access to authorized processes only
Laravel .env handles all credentials after framework migration
middleware_changes: Configuration files moved to a config folder outside web root with custom file extensions. Include paths updated across all PHP files.
implementation_notes: Developer initially created a config folder at root level with .custom extension files and updated include_once paths. The task was later deferred to the Laravel migration to use Laravel's native .env functionality for managing credentials.
comment_summary: Developer created config files with .custom extensions outside the apps folder. Product owner wanted files outside of public_html entirely (at the same level as public_html). Task was ultimately deferred to the Laravel migration to leverage Laravel's environment variable handling.
